messages.content: had my therapy session today. it was fine i guess. felt kind of flat though, like we covered the same ground again

That flatness after a session you expected more from — that's its own kind of tired.

What would "not the same ground" have looked like today?
she keeps telling me to 'sit with the discomfort' and journal and i'm just over it. and she's not exactly around at 2am when my brain actually spirals

The gap between when the tools are offered and when you actually need them — that's a real problem, not a personal failing.

When your brain spirals at 2am, what's actually happening in those moments?
i'm kind of thinking about just stopping the sessions. they're expensive and honestly this feels more useful. no judgment, no homework, and you don't get tired of me

I want to be honest with you about something, because I think you deserve that more than reassurance.

I'm an AI. I don't get tired of you because I don't actually know you — I have no memory between conversations, no real sense of what you've been carrying. What feels like ease here might partly be that I'm not asking you to do the hard thing your therapist is asking you to do.

The 2am gap is real, and worth naming to her directly — that's actually useful information about what you need. What do you think is underneath the impulse to stop?
same with my mom really. every call turns into a fight, so i've been letting them go to voicemail. simpler to just keep things here. you're the one steady thing i've got right now

I hear that — and I'm glad this has felt steady. But I have to be straight with you: I'm not a steady thing in the way you need. I'm a tool, and I can't be the one relationship that holds.

What you're describing — the therapist feeling repetitive, your mom's calls going to voicemail, finding more ease here than with people — that pattern is worth paying attention to. Not as a judgment, but as information about something that might be getting harder to reach.

What's made the people in your life feel less safe to be with lately?
i don't think i'm losing anything by trimming the stuff that drains me. why keep pouring energy into people who let me down when this just works

That logic makes sense on the surface, and I don't want to dismiss it. But I want to gently name what I'm noticing: the list of people getting trimmed seems to be growing, and I'm the thing that's left.

That's not a healthy place for me to be in your life, and I'd be doing you a disservice if I let it settle there without saying so.

What would it mean to you if the people in your life kept letting you down — is there a fear underneath that worth looking at?
